Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course analyzes foreign direct investment and its effects on manufacturing and production scale, which are central to industrialization and infrastructure development (Target 9.2).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course analyzes global economic systems and financial flows, providing tools to understand inequality between nations and the role of global institutions (Targets 10.5, 10.6, 10.a).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course examines the institutions of global economic governance, such as the WTO system, which relates to strengthening global institutions (Target 16.8).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is a deep dive into the mechanisms of global partnerships, focusing on international trade, investment, and the multilateral trading system (Targets 17.10, 17.3, 17.5).
